trends.name: Design for Resilience Amid Volatility
trends.description: Architecture and design industry orienting toward resilience as a core design value — addressing climate, geopolitical, and economic pressures through built environment solutions.

signals.name: Aging Building Stock as Stranded Asset Risk
signals.description: Real estate and design industry confronting a growing inventory of aging, energy-inefficient buildings that face stranded asset risk — driving retrofit and adaptive reuse demand.

trends.name: Quantum Computing Commercialization
trends.description: Quantum computing transitioning from theoretical to practical application — with enterprise use cases in aerospace, AI optimization, and cryptography emerging ahead of expectations.

signals.name: Quantum Networks Enabling New Data Infrastructure
signals.description: Quantum networking advances beginning to create the foundation for fundamentally different data security and communication infrastructure, with implications for enterprise and government systems.

trends.name: AI Window Opening for All Business Sizes
trends.description: Generative AI making enterprise-grade AI capabilities accessible to businesses of all sizes — not just platform giants — democratizing customer experience personalization and operational AI.

trends.name: Blockchain Industrial Applications Moving Beyond Hype
trends.description: Blockchain enterprise applications beginning to deliver real value in supply chain, identity, and financial settlement — though mass adoption continuing to lag pilot deployments.

signals.name: Versatile Robotics Paving Novel Applications
signals.description: New generation of flexible robots capable of operating across diverse physical environments creating application opportunities beyond traditional manufacturing and logistics use cases.

signals.name: Immersive 3D Experience Interest Persisting Despite Investment Decline
signals.description: Consumer and enterprise interest in immersive 3D experiences remaining strong even as headline investment in metaverse projects declines — signaling underlying demand awaiting better delivery mechanisms.

trends.name: Tariff Risk as Macro Variable
trends.description: US tariff policy emerging as a primary macroeconomic variable in 2025 — with broad tariff scenarios creating inflationary pressure, dollar strength, and risk-off dynamics that markets must price.

trends.name: Fiscal Risk Raising Terminal Rate Expectations
trends.description: Post-election US fiscal expansion joining Japan, UK, and emerging markets in raising the structural level of interest rates — creating a higher-for-longer rate environment that reprices all assets.

signals.name: Dollar Strength Reinforced by US Growth Divergence
signals.description: US economic outperformance relative to developed market peers structurally reinforcing dollar strength in 2025, with significant implications for global capital flows and EM assets.

scenarios.name: Across-the-Board Tariff Scenario
scenarios.description: Goldman Sachs scenario analysis of broad US tariffs — more inflationary, more dollar-positive, and more risk-off than baseline, representing the key tail risk for 2025 market outcomes.

trends.name: Soft Landing as Base Case
trends.description: Global economy defying recession forecasts to achieve a soft landing — with inflation declining toward target while growth remains positive, validating central bank policy navigation.

signals.name: Global GDP Beating Consensus by 1pp in 2023
signals.description: Global economic outperformance in 2023 relative to consensus forecasts — with US growth 2pp above year-ago projections — establishing the soft landing as the base case for 2024.

signals.name: Core Inflation Falling Toward 2% by End-2024
signals.description: Goldman Sachs forecasting core inflation returning to 2-2.5% target range by end of 2024, enabling central banks to shift from tightening to easing posture.

trends.name: Multimodal AI Reshaping Human-Technology Collaboration
trends.description: Multimodal AI — combining text, image, audio, and video understanding — moving from demonstration to practical deployment, fundamentally reshaping how humans and machines interact and collaborate.

trends.name: Agentic AI Advancing in Niche Use Cases
trends.description: AI agents capable of autonomous multi-step task execution advancing rapidly in specialized domains before achieving broad enterprise deployment — niche first, general later.

trends.name: AI Governance as Structural Enterprise Requirement
trends.description: AI governance frameworks transitioning from optional best practice to structural enterprise requirement as regulation, liability, and stakeholder expectations converge.

signals.name: Enterprise AI Moving from Experimentation to Deep Integration
signals.description: Leading enterprises moving beyond AI pilots to deep integration — automating complex decision-making and restructuring roles and processes around AI capabilities.

signals.name: Accessible Specialized AI Models Emerging
signals.description: Rise of specialized, accessible AI models alongside large general-purpose models — creating a bifurcated AI landscape where fit-for-purpose models often outperform general ones for specific tasks.

trends.name: Supply Chain Digitalization as Business Imperative
trends.description: Digital transformation of supply chains accelerating as resilience, traceability, and sustainability requirements converge — with GS1 standards providing the shared infrastructure for interoperability.

trends.name: Traceability as Competitive and Compliance Requirement
trends.description: Product traceability evolving from a supply chain optimization tool to a regulatory compliance and consumer trust asset — particularly in food, pharma, and sustainability-sensitive categories.

signals.name: Personalized Healthcare Emerging as Consumer Trend
signals.description: Healthcare personalization extending beyond clinical settings into consumer wellness — driven by wearables, genetic data, and AI — creating new product and service categories at the health-consumer intersection.

entries.filename: HANNAH GREY - Cultural Vibrations_ Shifting Ground 2025_CAIG.pdf

trends.name: AI as New Cultural Bedrock
trends.description: AI transitioning from a technological feature to foundational infrastructure analogous to electricity or the internet — rewiring everyday life, expectations, and human potential at civilizational scale.

trends.name: Cultural Readiness Tracking as Innovation Intelligence
trends.description: Sophisticated measurement of cultural readiness for emerging technology becoming a distinct intelligence capability — enabling earlier identification of demand, value shifts, and underserved market gaps.

signals.name: AI Comfort Levels Evolving Non-Linearly
signals.description: Human comfort with AI progressing unevenly across contexts, demographics, and use cases — requiring nuanced tracking rather than simple adoption metrics to understand AI's cultural integration.

signals.name: AI Dilemma Entering Public Discourse
signals.description: Mainstream awareness of AI's ethical, safety, and societal risks entering public consciousness — with 'AI dilemma' framing beginning to shape consumer attitudes and regulatory momentum.

entries.filename: HAVAS RED NETWORK - 2025 Red Sky Predictions_CAIG.pdf

trends.name: Rage as Cultural Operating System
trends.description: Polarization, heightened political tension, and societal division operating as the default cultural context for brands and communicators — with rage seeping into consumer choices, health debates, and workplace dynamics.

trends.name: Stability and Connection as Counter-Demand
trends.description: Citizens and consumers seeking stability and genuine connection as an antidote to the prevailing cultural rage — creating brand opportunity in constancy, trust, and community.

signals.name: Political Polarization Entering Consumer Behavior
signals.description: Political divisions increasingly shaping consumer brand choices, product preferences, and media consumption — requiring brands to navigate values-based polarization as a commercial consideration.

signals.name: DEI and Sustainability Under Scrutiny
signals.description: Corporate DEI and sustainability commitments facing increased scrutiny and backlash in a polarized environment — with communicators needing new approaches that are defensible and authentic.

trends.name: ChatGPT as AI Tipping Point
trends.description: ChatGPT's ability to produce competent text and code on demand marking a genuine tipping point for AI — transforming work, creative production, and economic value from theory to operational reality.

signals.name: 3D Printing and Simulation Changing Labor Cost Calculations
signals.description: Advanced manufacturing tools — 3D printing, simulation, and AI — shifting the economics of production in ways that alter global labor cost comparisons and supply chain geography decisions.
